basics.name: Maya Mikelson
basics.label: Ph.D. Student
basics.Research Interests: Conflict and cooperation, security studies, militarization, international interventions, human rights

education.area: Political Science, minor in Data Science

work.name: Inter-university Consortium for Political and Social Research, University of Michigan
work.position: Research Assistant under Professor Christopher J. Fariss
highlights: Developed latent variable models in R with the Stan library to calculate the likelihood of signing treaties in every year between 1966 and 2022.
Coded a web scraper to analyze treaty ratification data from the UN Treaty Body database.

work.name: Ralph Bunche Summer Institute (RBSI), Duke University
work.position: Researcher under Professors Paula D. McClain and David A. Siegel
highlights: Developed independent research project on the impact of third-party military interventions on human rights and government repression during intrastate conflict using a Heckman Probit Selection Model.
Completed graduate-level courses in Race and American Politics and Probability and Regression.

work.name: NSF REU Accountability, Behavior & Conflict in Democratic Politics, Vanderbilt University
work.position: Researcher under Professor Emily Hencken Ritter
highlights: Developed an independent empirical project analyzing the relationship between militarization and interstate conflict that was presented in a poster session at APSA 2023, Los Angeles.
Compiled historical sources into case studies examining race riots and police violence in the United States.

work.name: Decision Desk HQ
work.position: Data Editor and Election Night Reporter
highlights: Decision Desk HQ reports election results from county election officials. Responsible for a team of editors checking election data results for errors, communicating, and resolving data errors.
Promoted from Election Night Reporter to Data Editor after four years.

work.name: The Michigan Daily
highlights: Researched, wrote, and edited data journalism stories and visuals.
Led collaboration with other sections of the Daily to produce articles.
Conducted data analyses and created graphics for publication.
Represented Michigan at competitions around the United States.
Researched antitrust, legal personhood, and nuclear weapons policy.
Researched energy policy in Afghanistan after the Taliban took control.

awards.title: Nominated for Sweetland Prize for Excellence in Upper-Level Writing
3rd Place, Best Interactive Infographic by Pinnacle College Media Awards
awards.summary: How Walkable Is Your Block? The Michigan Daily

publications.name: Measuring Militarization and the Link with Interstate Conflict
publications.publisher: Senior Honors Thesis, University of Michigan / Midwest Political Science Association Conference
publications.summary: Poster presentation at the Midwest Political Science Association Conference, April 2025

publications.name: Third-Party Military Interventions, Government Repression, and Human Rights
publications.summary: Ralph Bunche Summer Institute Paper, June 2024

publications.name: Militarization and Interstate Armed Conflict
publications.publisher: American Political Science Association Annual Meeting
publications.summary: Poster presentation at the American Political Science Association Annual Meeting, September 2023
